title: Multi-Provider Strategy — DeepSeek + Claude Code Subscription
description: Turns two proven spikes into shippable features: DeepSeek as a first-class provider via the existing openai-compat adapter, and a claude-code subscription provider for prompt-only roles. Also fixes an eslint peer-dependency conflict that breaks npm install, adds role-aware provider fallback for claude-code, and documents the provider capability matrix. anthropic stays the default.

clarificationQuestions.question: Should deepseek-reasoner/deepseek-chat (deprecating 2026-07-24) be mapped, or only the v4 line?

options.label: A
options.description: Map only the v4 line (deepseek, deepseek-v4-pro, deepseek-v4-flash).

clarificationQuestions.recommendation: A — avoid shipping soon-dead aliases (PRD Open Questions leans v4-only).

clarificationQuestions.question: For claude-code, should we expose a bober->--model mapping or pass the model string through verbatim?

options.label: A
options.description: Pass the model string through verbatim to claude --model.

clarificationQuestions.recommendation: A — the spike already passes verbatim (claude-code.ts:137-139); lowest risk.

clarificationQuestions.question: Should the openai-compat preflight be a hard error or a warning when an openai-family provider is configured but openai is not installed?

options.label: A
options.description: Warning + actionable hint; users may install openai later.

clarificationQuestions.recommendation: A — PRD US-004 says 'prints an actionable hint'; keep the existing call-time throw as backstop.

clarificationQuestions.question: Is a generic openai-compat + DEEPSEEK_API_KEY special-case acceptable, or should key-env be configurable per endpoint?

options.label: A
options.description: Special-case DEEPSEEK_API_KEY keyed on the api.deepseek.com endpoint.

options.label: B
options.description: Make key-env configurable per endpoint (future multi-vendor).

clarificationQuestions.recommendation: A — PRD FR-3 specifies exactly this; per-vendor key-env is a Non-Goal.

resolvedClarifications.answer: Map only the v4 line. PRD Open Questions and task instructions both direct 'map only the deepseek v4 line'.
Pass the model string through verbatim. src/providers/claude-code.ts lines 137-139 already do args.push('--model', model) with no expansion.
Warning/actionable hint before the first model call (not a hard abort); the existing call-time throw in openai.ts:284-286 stays as the backstop. PRD US-004 AC and Open Questions support warning.
Special-case DEEPSEEK_API_KEY keyed on api.deepseek.com endpoint, per PRD FR-3. Per-vendor configurable key-env is explicitly deferred.

assumptions: anthropic remains the default provider; DeepSeek and claude-code are opt-in (PRD Goals).

Both spikes are already proven: src/providers/claude-code.ts and scripts/spike-deepseek.mjs exist on branch spike/claude-code-provider; DeepSeek works through the existing openai-compat adapter with zero new adapter code (PRD Empirical findings).
Only the deepseek v4 line (deepseek, deepseek-v4-pro, deepseek-v4-flash) is mapped (Q1).
claude-code passes its model string to claude --model verbatim (Q2); claude-code.ts:137-139.
The openai-absent preflight is a warning/hint, not a hard error (Q3); the existing call-time throw in openai.ts:284-286 is the backstop.
DEEPSEEK_API_KEY is special-cased on the api.deepseek.com endpoint (Q4, PRD FR-3).
Provider tests mock execa (claude-code) and the openai client (deepseek) so CI needs no keys/binaries/subscription (PRD Technical Considerations).
Smoke scripts are key/binary-gated and excluded from npm test (PRD US-008, FR-9).
Tool-using roles are curator, generator, evaluator, code-reviewer; prompt-only roles are planner and researcher (PRD Provider Capability Matrix).
.env is already gitignored (.gitignore lines 10-12), so the no-key-persistence rule needs documentation, not new ignore entries.
outOfScope: Making claude-code work for tool-using roles (would require driving tools through Claude Code's native loop via custom MCP).
Changing the default provider away from anthropic.
Adding the Claude Agent SDK (@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk) as a dependency.
Auto-installing openai on the user's behalf.
Bundling/committing any API key, or a key manager/secrets vault.
Cost dashboards or usage metering UI.

features.title: Fix eslint peer-dependency conflict
features.description: Upgrade eslint to ^10 so @eslint/js@^10 is satisfied and npm install succeeds without --legacy-peer-deps.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: eslint devDependency is ^10 and npm install exits 0 with no ERESOLVE on a clean tree.
AC2: npm run lint, npm run build, and npm test all pass with no lint-rule changes (only version + config shims).

features.title: DeepSeek shorthand in model-resolver
features.description: Map deepseek, deepseek-v4-pro, deepseek-v4-flash to openai-compat at https://api.deepseek.com.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: resolveProviderModel('deepseek-v4-pro') returns provider openai-compat, modelId deepseek-v4-pro, endpoint https://api.deepseek.com.
AC2: deepseek and deepseek-v4-flash resolve to the same endpoint with their respective modelIds; existing ollama/ and anthropic shorthands are unchanged.

features.title: DeepSeek API-key resolution and validation
features.description: Add a DEEPSEEK_API_KEY env fallback to the openai-compat key chain and a DeepSeek-specific missing-key error when the endpoint is api.deepseek.com.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: Key resolves from providerConfig.apiKey else process.env.DEEPSEEK_API_KEY for the deepseek endpoint.
AC2: validateApiKey throws a DEEPSEEK_API_KEY-named error when the deepseek endpoint has no key, instead of the Ollama no-key skip.

features.title: openai optional-peer preflight and missing-package error
features.description: Verify the missing-openai-package error fires and add a preflight warning when an openai-family provider is configured but openai is absent.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: The openai adapter throws an error containing 'npm install openai' when import('openai') fails (test mocks the import).
AC2: A preflight emits a 'npm install openai' hint only when an openai-family provider is configured and openai is absent; silent otherwise.

features.title: Promote claude-code subscription provider
features.description: Wire ClaudeCodeAdapter into createClient; no API key, preflight the claude binary, honor binary/timeoutMs overrides, keep the tools-guard throw.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: createClient('claude-code', ...) returns a ClaudeCodeAdapter and validateApiKey requires no key but errors when the claude binary is absent.
AC2: chat maps the mocked execa JSON result/usage into ChatResponse and throws when tools are passed.

features.title: Role-aware provider fallback for claude-code
features.description: At config load, tool roles on claude-code fall back to another configured provider, or hard-error naming the role when none exists; prompt roles are unaffected; decisions are logged.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: A tool role resolving to claude-code with another provider configured uses the other provider; with none, config load fails naming the role.
AC2: planner/researcher on claude-code are allowed and each role's resolved provider is logged.

features.title: Documentation — provider matrix, costs, ToS
features.description: Update README and add docs/providers.md with the capability matrix, costs, ToS, prerequisites, and the no-key-persistence rule.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: README Supported Providers contains the capability matrix and docs/providers.md has copy-paste config per provider.
AC2: Docs state agent-bober never persists keys; markdownlint passes.

features.title: Key-gated provider smoke scripts
features.description: Keep deepseek and add claude-code smoke scripts that skip when key/binary absent and are excluded from npm test.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: Smoke scripts skip (exit 0) without DEEPSEEK_API_KEY / claude binary and make no network call.
AC2: npm test does not execute any smoke script.

integrationPoints: DeepSeek API at https://api.deepseek.com via the openai-compat adapter
local claude CLI in headless mode (claude -p --output-format json) for subscription billing
techNotes.dataModel: No persistent data model. Provider resolution maps config (model/provider/endpoint/providerConfig) to an LLMClient adapter. Role->provider resolution computed at config load.
securityConsiderations: Never persist or commit API keys; .env stays gitignored.
claude-code uses subscription auth — no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY read.
Smoke scripts key/binary-gated and excluded from CI.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: npm install on a clean clone succeeds with exit 0 and no ERESOLVE without --legacy-peer-deps.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Clean rm -rf node_modules && npm install exits 0 with no ERESOLVE in output.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: No API key is ever written to a committed file; .env remains gitignored.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: git check-ignore .env returns .env; grep source tree for hardcoded keys finds none.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: Provider tests run with no keys/binaries by mocking execa and the openai client.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: npm test exits 0 with no real network or process spawn (mocks asserted).

constraints: ESM everywhere, .js import extensions, import type.
No SDK imports outside provider adapter files.
Zod for config validation; no hand-rolled validation.
